chunks.text: Agile Design of Efficient Processing Technologies (ADEPT) aims to dramatically improve computing capability by reducing the cost and risk of designing custom silicon for new application areas. Their integrated 5-year research mission cuts across applications, programming systems, architecture, and hardware design and verification methodologies. Agile Robotics And Perception Lab (ARPL) performs fundamental and applied research in robotics autonomy, creating agile autonomous machines that navigate using on-board sensors in unstructured environments without external infrastructure like GPS. Algorithms and Computing for Education (ACE) Lab brings together interdisciplinary individuals interested in research at the intersection of education and computing, developing novel educational software for traditional, online, and hybrid learning.

Arts Research Center (ARC) is a think tank for the arts, acting as a hub where artists, scholars, curators, and civic arts leaders from various disciplines gather and learn. Berkeley Air & Space Center (BASC) advances UC Berkeley's mission of education, research, and public service, leveraging campus academic excellence in emerging fields. Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR) brings together researchers in computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, and robotics, with over two dozen faculty and more than a hundred graduate students pursuing fundamental advances and cross-cutting themes like multi-modal deep learning and human-compatible AI.

Berkeley Audio-visual Signal processing and Communication Systems (BASiCS) focuses on peer-to-peer networks, multimedia processing, information and coding theory, and collaborative signal processing, designing robust large-scale systems with unreliable entities. Berkeley Benchmarking and Optimization Group (BeBOP) studies software performance tuning and hardware design implications, replacing the Berkeley Institute for Performance Studies. Berkeley Center for Computational Imaging (BCCI) brings together interdisciplinary researchers for end-to-end imaging system design, from sensor hardware to reconstruction algorithms, impacting scientific research, clinical care, and consumer products like MRI and VR.

Berkeley Center for New Media (BCNM) explores how new media transforms perception, learning, communication, and experience, emphasizing humanities and public interest from cross-disciplinary perspectives. Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI) advances decentralization and web3 science, technology, and education for a responsible digital economy, involving faculty from computer science, finance/economics, and law. Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD) researches deep automotive perception by integrating vision and vehicles, merging deep learning with automotive perception for computer vision applications in automotive industries.

Berkeley Education Alliance for Research in Singapore (BEARS) pursues research in power generation and building efficiency, focusing on information technology, sensors, solar energy, engineering, construction, economics, architecture, and human behavior. Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research (BETR) Center is a hub for physical electronics research, facilitating long-term collaborations with companies and access to faculty and students building future electronic devices. Berkeley Equity and Access in Algorithms, Mechanisms, and Optimization (BEAAMO) works on algorithms and AI with a focus on inequality and distributive justice, using techniques from algorithms, machine learning, and human-computer interaction to impact marginalized communities.

Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS) brings together domain experts from life, social, and physical sciences with methodological experts from computer science and statistics to foster collaboration across research fields. Berkeley Institute of Design (BID) spans computer science, architecture, industrial and mechanical engineering as a new design discipline. Berkeley Laboratory for Automation Science and Engineering (AUTOLab), directed by Professor Ken Goldberg, researches robotics and automation with projects in networked telerobotics, computer-assisted surgery, automated manufacturing, and new media art forms.

Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences (BLISS) focuses on communications, information and coding theory, optimization, statistics, and control. Berkeley Laboratory for Usable and Experimental Security (BLUES) researches computer security, online privacy, and human-computer interaction to understand privacy and security decisions and build more usable systems. Berkeley Power and Energy Center (BPEC) furthers research and education in power and energy through industry engagement, creating an ecosystem for academic and industry partners to share ideas and advance technology.

Berkeley Quantum Information and Computation Center (BQIC) investigates quantum information processing at the foundations of computer science and quantum mechanics, with quantum computers providing exponential speedups for tasks like integer factorization and secure communication. Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center (BSAC) researches sensors and MEMS using integrated-circuit technology. Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC) focuses on highly-integrated CMOS implementations with low energy consumption and advanced communication algorithms, fabricated with state-of-the-art processes.

Biomimetic Millisystems Lab harnesses animal locomotion features to enable high-performance mobile millirobots. California Institute for Energy and Environment (CIEE) conducts public-interest energy research to meet California's energy goals, with the Electric Grid Research group co-directed by Dr. von Meier and Dr. Merwin Brown, and consolidates with Berkeley Energy and Climate Institute. Center for Augmented Cognition (CAC) supports research on new computing paradigms, human cognition modeling, human-computer interaction, and human-robot collaboration through augmented and virtual reality technologies.

Center for Automation and Learning for Medical Robotics (Cal-MR) advances automation and machine learning to improve robots and devices for medicine and surgery. Center for Computational Biology (CCB) advances understanding between computational and biological sciences through interdisciplinary research and education. Center for Energy Efficient Electronics Science (E3S) is an NSF STC focusing on reducing energy consumption in electronic systems by orders of magnitude. Center for Human Compatible Artificial Intelligence (CHAI) aims to reorient AI research towards provably beneficial systems.

Center for Information Technology Research in the Interest of Society - The Banatao Institute (CITRIS) sponsors collaborative IT research to solve grand-challenge social and commercial problems affecting Californians' quality of life, including energy efficiency, transportation, environmental monitoring, seismic safety, education, cultural research, and health care. Center for Integrated Access Networks (CIAN) creates transformative technologies for optical access networks to aggregate applications cost-effectively. Center for Interdisciplinary Biological Inspiration in Education and Research (CiBER) focuses on learning from nature to inspire engineering design and train collaborative scientists and engineers, funded by NSF.

Center for Neural Engineering & Prostheses (CNEP) integrates engineering with neurosciences to develop technology restoring sensory, motor, and cognitive function in patients with neurological conditions. Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB) addresses conceptual challenges from interdisciplinary technologies focusing on data, inferences, and decisions to understand interactions with real-world phenomena. CITRIS Connected Communities focuses on IT affordances to enhance learning, practice, and governance communities through experimental online platforms and novel hardware and software systems.

CITRIS Health develops transformative IT solutions to improve health and wellness, building on a history of addressing critical health challenges. CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR) supports robotics research from UC campuses on themes like Cloud Robotics, Deep Learning, Human-Centric Automation, and Bio-Inspired Robotics. CITRIS Sustainable Infrastructure uses IT to weave transportation, energy, and water systems into a sustainable fabric for resiliency and climate change. CITRIS Women In Tech Initiative (WITI) addresses the tech industry's diversity challenge by integrating research and action with industry partners.

Computer Graphics at Berkeley (B-CAM) was formerly the Berkeley Computer Animation & Modeling Group. Electronic Materials Program at LBNL (E-Mat) advances semiconductor materials science for DOE mission technologies. EPIC Data lab develops low-code and no-code interfaces for data work using predictive programming techniques. FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality sponsors research in VR, AR, and AI and serves as a hub for cross-disciplinary deployment.

Foundations Of Resilient CybEr-physical Systems (FORCES) protects critical infrastructure from attack and ensures robust operation of cyber-physical systems in energy delivery, transportation, and building energy management. Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute (HWNI) is the nexus for multidisciplinary neuroscience research and the Berkeley BRAIN Initiative, using approaches from biophysics to computer science to understand brain function and treat diseases. Human-Assistive Robotic Technologies Lab (HART) models human capabilities to develop robust human-centered systems for various applications.

Hyperpolarized MRI Technology Resource Center uses Dynamic Nuclear Polarization for signal enhancements in carbon-13 compounds to detect metabolic products in vivo. Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems Center (iCyPhy) develops engineering techniques for products combining software, hardware, and mechanical components, partnering with UC Berkeley, Caltech, and companies like United Technologies and IBM. Industrial Engineering and Operations Research (IEOR) invents tools for design, analysis, risk management, and decision-making in complex systems like supply chains and healthcare.

Institute of Transportation Studies (ITS) develops innovations influencing movement of people and goods for sustainability and quality of life. Integrated Modeling Process and Computation for Technology (IMPACT) introduces innovative process steps, sensors, and control strategies to make IC manufacturing factories more efficient. International Computer Science Institute (ICSI) is a nonprofit center for computer science research with a focus on international collaboration. Jacobs Institute for Design Innovation (JIDI) is an interdisciplinary hub for design and technology exploration.

Kavli Center for Ethics, Science, and the Public (KAVLI) breaks down barriers between sciences, humanities, and the public to understand ethical implications of science and technology. Laboratory for Automation Science and Engineering (AUTOLAB), directed by Professor Ken Goldberg, researches robotics and automation with projects in Cloud Robotics, Deep Reinforcement Learning, and more. Marvell NanoLab is a shared research center with cleanroom facilities for micro- and nano-fabrication, serving over 100 Principal Investigators.

Multiscale System Center (MuSyC) designs Distributed Sense and Control Systems. Networked Systems Lab (NETSYS) addresses networking and systems issues like congestion control, data analytics, and Software-Defined Networking. NSF Challenge Institute for Quantum Computation (CIQC) focuses on quantum computing challenges in algorithms, verifiable advantage, and scaling quantum systems. Partners for Advanced Transit and Highways (PATH) develops foundations for advanced technologies to improve California's surface transportation systems.

Quantum Architecture Research explores structures and technologies for large-scale quantum computing in collaboration with UC Davis, MIT, and University of Washington. Robotics and Intelligent Machines Laboratory in EECS spans topics from microscopic to autonomous systems and human-robot interaction. Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC) promotes fundamental research on computer science foundations and expands horizons through computational lenses on other disciplines. Sky Computing Lab leverages distributed systems and machine learning to decouple services from specific cloud choices for seamless multi-cloud applications.

SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory operates major facilities like the Linear Coherent Light Source and programs in energy research, biological, chemical, materials, and astrophysical sciences. Software Principles for Advanced Networking (SPAN) makes networks more amenable to innovation with software-defined networks for rapid deployment of new features. SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE) democratizes design and programming of domain-specific computing at scale for various applications. Synthetic Biology Institute (SBI) develops standards and technologies for producing biological systems to benefit society in energy, materials, and pharmaceuticals.

Technology Competitiveness and Industrial Policy Center (TCIP) provides scholarly advice to policy-makers for leadership in technology R&D and advanced manufacturing for economic prosperity and national security. Tele-Immersion enables cooperative interaction between geographically separated people in the same virtual environment. Verified Human Interfaces, Control, and Learning for Semi-Autonomous Systems (VeHICaL) develops verified co-design of interfaces and control for human cyber-physical systems with provable guarantees. Video and Image Processing Lab (VIP Lab) focuses on theories and applications of signal, image, and video processing, including wireless multimedia and 3D modeling.

Visual Computing Lab (VCL) is not described in detail in the provided text. Other entries like 'Carmena Lab website' and 'Retired' are listed without descriptions, indicating potential placeholder or outdated content that should be excluded from the cleaned corpus as they lack substantive information for chunking and QA generation.
